title: blocks data model and migration
description: Add the `blocks` table (`id, tab_id, tipo, conteudo_json, ordem`) to the Drizzle schema, storing each top-level Tiptap node as a typed block row.
acceptanceCriteria: `blocks` table exists with `id, tab_id (fk -> tabs.id), tipo (text, constrained to the 8 MVP block types), conteudo_json (text, storing serialized JSON), ordem (integer)`
`tipo` values are constrained to `heading | paragraph | list | code | image | table | callout | component-embed` (via a check constraint or application-level validation, documented either way)
Migration applies cleanly on top of prior migrations
Deleting a tab cascades to its blocks
Drizzle query layer exposes typed insert/select helpers that parse `conteudo_json` back into a `Block['content']` shape from `packages/schema`

steps.details: Define `blocks` with `id`, `tabId: text('tab_id').notNull().references(() => tabs.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' })`, `tipo: text('tipo').notNull()`, `conteudoJson: text('conteudo_json').notNull()`, `ordem: integer('ordem').notNull()`.
Either add a SQLite `CHECK` constraint listing the 8 valid values, or validate at the application layer with a zod enum shared from `packages/schema`. Document the choice in a code comment.
Run `drizzle-kit generate`, confirm the cascade-on-delete-tab behavior and (if used) the check constraint SQL.

In `apps/server/src/db/blocks.ts`, add helpers that `JSON.parse`/`JSON.stringify` `conteudo_json` and type the result as `Block['content']` from `@systembook/schema`, so calling code never handles raw JSON strings directly.
Insert a block of each of the 8 types with representative `conteudo_json`, read them back via the typed helper, and confirm shapes round-trip correctly.

technicalNotes: Prefer application-level zod validation over a SQLite CHECK constraint for `tipo` if the block type set is expected to grow post-MVP — CHECK constraints require a migration to alter, while a shared zod enum only requires a code change plus a normal deploy.
